channel.title: Blog on Maciej Workiewicz
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Maciej Workiewicz

item.title: Human-AI collaboration: The power of mental models
item.description: An experiment showing how LLMs fail at spatial reasoning with modified chessboards, highlighting the importance of mental models in strategic thinking.

item.title: Fresh thinking: Why opening your windows could boost your remote work performance
item.description: How indoor CO2 levels affect cognitive performance and why remote workers should pay attention to ventilation.

item.title: Online Teaching During the Pandemic: Insights and Lessons Learned
item.description: Insights from student surveys and personal experience about what worked and what didn't in online teaching during the pandemic.

item.title: Merging Minds and Machines: A Manifesto for the AI-Enhanced Workforce
item.description: A mini-manifesto for companies seeking to benefit from AI technology, based on the tension between exploration and exploitation.

item.title: The academic job market presents a more diverse set of choices in 2022
item.description: Analysis of the increasing geographic diversity of top management research institutions worldwide.

item.title: Teaching AI And Strategy
item.description: Insights from teaching a course on strategy in the age of AI.

item.title: How to move to online teaching (fast)
item.description: A comprehensive guide to quickly transitioning to online teaching, covering software, hardware, and best practices.

item.title: Research and teaching in business schools: Can the two go together
item.description: Data analysis showing the positive correlation between research output and teaching quality in business schools.

item.title: The Improving State of Management Research in Europe (An Academic Post)
item.description: Data showing how European and Asian business schools have been catching up in management research output.

item.title: A reading list
item.description: A curated reading list spanning business strategy, decision-making, geopolitics, AI, philosophy of science, and more.

item.title: On the pleasures of wasting time or on recreating March 1991 original code
item.description: Recreating the famous March 1991 Exploration and Exploitation model in Python.

item.title: GitHub: exploring the benefits and limits of the boss-less organizational form
item.description: Discussion of GitHub's transition from a boss-less structure to traditional hierarchy, published in the Journal of Organization Design.

item.title: Behavioral Strategy Wiki site launches
item.description: Launch announcement for the Behavioral Strategy Wiki, an interactive literature matrix for the behavioral strategy community.

item.title: Mapping James G. March's ideas
item.description: Literature and theory maps of James G. March's vast body of work.

item.title: Mapping the ideas of Professor Sidney Winter
item.description: A literature map of Professor Sidney Winter's key ideas on routines, capabilities, and evolutionary economics.

item.title: Readings on flat management
item.description: A curated list of readings on self-organizing and flat management structures.

item.title: Is having two bosses like not having bosses at all?
item.description: Exploring the parallels and differences between matrix organizations with multiple bosses and boss-less organizations.

item.title: At Zappos 210 employees say "no" to "no bosses"
item.description: Commentary on Zappos' transition to holacracy and the 14% of employees who chose to leave.

item.title: "Unfortunately god gave all the easy problems to the physicists" - James G. March
item.description: A brief reflection on the difficulty of studying organizations, with a link to a brilliant comment by James G. March.
